IC/IR guarantee: exact IC under the model; IR via optimal auction construction
main limitation: Does not solve general multi-bidder multi-item combinatorial revenue-optimal auctions
relation to ourwork: Classical baseline: explains why multi-item revenue-optimal design remains hard and why ourwork should not claim full optimality

IC/IR guarantee: exact DSIC and IR under standard assumptions
handles combinatorial items?: Yes in principle, if allocation optimization is solved
main limitation: Revenue can be suboptimal; static VCG does not adapt parameters for revenue
relation to ourwork: Ourwork inherits the VCG-style externality logic through AMA but tunes affine parameters for revenue

IC/IR guarantee: AMA form is DSIC when weights are positive
handles combinatorial items?: Relevant to combinatorial settings but assumptions matter
main limitation: Not a revenue-learning method; characterization does not solve dynamic tuning
relation to ourwork: Provides the structural reason AMA can be used as the exact-DSIC shell

paper: Likhodedov and Sandholm (2004/2005); Sandholm and Likhodedov (2015), AMA revenue boosting
learning method: search / offline optimization of AMA parameters
main limitation: Offline/static distributional optimization; not an RL policy over repeated market states
relation to ourwork: Closest classical precursor to tuning AMA parameters for revenue

paper: Dutting et al. (2019), Optimal Auctions through Deep Learning / RegretNet
IC/IR guarantee: approximate IC via empirical regret; IR built into architecture
learning method: deep learning with augmented Lagrangian regret minimization
main limitation: High flexibility but not exact DSIC by construction; regret is empirical/approximate
relation to ourwork: Main contrast: ourwork sacrifices arbitrary mechanism flexibility to preserve per-round exact DSIC through AMA

paper: RochetNet / MenuNet (Dutting et al.; Shen et al.)
IC/IR guarantee: strategyproof by construction in target setting
main limitation: Does not directly cover general multi-bidder combinatorial auctions
relation to ourwork: Shows architecture-level truthfulness is possible, but does not address repeated AMA parameter tuning

paper: Curry, Sandholm, and Dickerson (2022), Differentiable Economics for Randomized AMAs
IC/IR guarantee: always strategyproof by AMA/lottery AMA structure; IR in mechanism design setting
main limitation: AMA may not represent optimal mechanism; focus is static training rather than repeated RL adaptation
relation to ourwork: Very close near neighbor: ourwork should position itself as dynamic/RL tuning of AMA parameters, not first AMA learning

auction setting: multi-item auctions; contextual and non-contextual settings
mechanism class: neural generation of AMA parameters and allocation menu
IC/IR guarantee: exact DSIC/IR due to AMA properties
main limitation: Focuses on scalable static DSIC auction design, not dynamic repeated revenue tuning
relation to ourwork: Shows exact-DSIC neural AMA design already exists; ourwork's gap must be dynamic/RL

mechanism class: Virtual Valuations Combinatorial Auctions, a subset of AMAs
main limitation: Deterministic/static design; not a repeated RL mechanism
relation to ourwork: Another exact-DSIC static AMA-family baseline to compare against

auction setting: single-item and multi-item auctions; correlated bidder valuations
mechanism class: AMA plus correlation-aware payment term depending on other bidders' valuations
IC/IR guarantee: DSIC preserved; IR handled as constraint/post-processing
main limitation: Targets correlated valuation expressiveness; not a dynamic RL framework
relation to ourwork: Important limitation/future work: ourwork's classic AMA may be weak under correlated valuations

paper: Athey and Segal (2013), An Efficient Dynamic Mechanism
auction setting: general dynamic environments with private information
IC/IR guarantee: ex post IC or Bayesian IC under assumptions; budget balance under independent types
handles combinatorial items?: General allocation, not specifically combinatorial auction revenue learning
main limitation: Focuses on efficiency and budget balance, not deep RL revenue tuning
relation to ourwork: Shows dynamic IC is subtle; ourwork should distinguish per-round DSIC from full dynamic IC

paper: Shen et al. (2017), Reinforcement Mechanism Design
mechanism class: GSP-like auction with learned reserve prices
IC/IR guarantee: Not primarily exact DSIC; data-driven bidder behavior model
main limitation: Domain-specific and not exact-DSIC combinatorial AMA
relation to ourwork: Shows RL has already been used for auction mechanism parameters, so ourwork should not claim first RL auction mechanism

paper: Qiu et al. (2022), Learning Dynamic Mechanisms in Unknown Environments
auction setting: dynamic mechanism design in unknown episodic MDP
IC/IR guarantee: truthfulness/efficiency connected to dynamic VCG; regret guarantees
learning method: online RL with linear function approximation
handles combinatorial items?: General dynamic allocation; not focused on combinatorial revenue AMA
main limitation: Focuses on learning dynamic VCG in unknown MDPs, not revenue-oriented AMA tuning
relation to ourwork: Closest dynamic VCG/RL theory neighbor; helps define what ourwork is not

paper: Leon and Etesami (2025), Online Learning for Dynamic VCG Mechanism
auction setting: sequential auctions in unknown infinite-horizon MDP
IC/IR guarantee: approximately satisfies efficiency, truthfulness, and IR with learning guarantees
handles combinatorial items?: Sequential auctions; not specifically AMA combinatorial revenue tuning
main limitation: Emphasis on dynamic VCG and efficiency/regret, not learned affine revenue parameters
relation to ourwork: Must be cited to avoid overclaiming dynamic RL mechanism novelty

paper: Ravindranath et al. (2024), Deep RL for Sequential Combinatorial Auctions
IC/IR guarantee: sequential mechanisms have strategyproofness guarantees in their setting
learning method: DRL leveraging first-order gradients; compared with PPO/SAC
handles combinatorial items?: Yes; scales to large agent/item counts
main limitation: Does not frame the mechanism as per-round AMA parameter tuning; exact guarantee depends on their sequential mechanism class
relation to ourwork: Most important RL + sequential combinatorial auction comparator

mechanism class: Affine Maximizer Auction with RL-tuned parameters; proposed HRL controller
IC/IR guarantee: per-round exact DSIC/IR if parameters fixed before bids and weights positive; not full dynamic IC
learning method: Flat DRL / proposed hierarchical RL over AMA parameters
handles combinatorial items?: Yes for small n,m via exact allocation enumeration
main limitation: Current evidence mainly Flat DRL; HRL advantage needs experiments; cross-round IC and scalability remain open
relation to ourwork: Position as dynamic revenue tuning inside exact-DSIC AMA parameter space
